name: ADP

certificateAuth.notes: SSL certificate-based mutual TLS authentication required. Certificate is registered with ADP during app setup. Both client_id/client_secret AND certificate are needed.

auth.notes: Uses OAuth 2.0 client_credentials grant with certificate-based mutual TLS. No authorize URL because it's server-to-server (no user consent flow). Scopes are configured during ADP Marketplace app registration, not in OAuth requests.

api.rateLimits: Per-integration profile rate limiting. Rate is per-second (per-minute/60). Concurrency limits per client per integration profile. 429 returned when exceeded.

sdk.notes: No official npm package. ADP provides Postman collections and sample payloads at github.com/adpllc/marketplace-sample-payloads. Use standard HTTP clients with mutual TLS.

webhooks.notes: ADP uses an event-based model. Events are stored in ADP's event manager database. Consumers poll for new events rather than receiving push notifications. Subscribe to data change events via ADP Marketplace configuration.

sandbox.notes: ADP provides a controlled UAT environment with sample data at uat-api.adp.com / uat-accounts.adp.com. Access requires ADP Marketplace partner registration.

needs_verification: Exact rate limit numbers per integration profile
Complete list of available event types beyond worker lifecycle
Whether SCIM is supported for any ADP product
Whether push webhooks (vs polling) are available in newer API versions
